name: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
description: A specialized program focusing on AI/ML algorithms, neural networks, deep learning, computer vision, natural language processing, and ethical considerations in intelligent systems.

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence
subjects.description: Foundations of artificial intelligence including intelligent agents, problem solving, search algorithms, knowledge representation, reasoning under uncertainty, and AI ethics.
learning_outcomes: Design intelligent agents for various problem domains
Implement search algorithms including A*, BFS, and DFS
Represent knowledge using first-order logic and ontologies
Apply Bayesian networks for probabilistic reasoning
subjects.industry_relevance: AI is the driving force behind automation, recommendation systems, and intelligent decision-making across all industries.

subjects.name: Machine Learning
subjects.description: Comprehensive introduction to machine learning algorithms including regression, classification, clustering, dimensionality reduction, and ensemble methods with hands-on implementations.
learning_outcomes: Implement linear and logistic regression models from scratch
Build classification models using SVM, decision trees, and random forests
Apply dimensionality reduction using PCA and t-SNE
Evaluate and tune models using cross-validation and hyperparameter optimization
subjects.industry_relevance: Machine learning expertise is in high demand for predictive analytics, recommendation engines, and automated decision systems.

subjects.name: Deep Learning
subjects.description: Advanced study of neural network architectures including CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, transformers, GANs, and autoencoders. Covers TensorFlow and PyTorch frameworks for building deep learning models.
learning_outcomes: Design and train convolutional neural networks for image tasks
Build sequence models using RNNs, LSTMs, and transformers
Implement generative models including GANs and VAEs
Deploy deep learning models using TensorFlow Serving or ONNX
subjects.industry_relevance: Deep learning powers breakthroughs in computer vision, NLP, autonomous vehicles, and healthcare diagnostics.

subjects.name: Reinforcement Learning
subjects.description: Study of reinforcement learning paradigms including Markov decision processes, Q-learning, policy gradients, deep reinforcement learning, and multi-agent systems.
learning_outcomes: Formulate problems as Markov decision processes
Implement Q-learning and SARSA algorithms
Train deep RL agents using policy gradient methods
Apply RL to robotics control and game playing environments
subjects.industry_relevance: Reinforcement learning is crucial for robotics, game AI, autonomous systems, and optimization problems in logistics.

subjects.name: Computer Vision
subjects.description: Study of techniques for image processing, feature extraction, object detection, segmentation, and 3D reconstruction using traditional and deep learning approaches.
learning_outcomes: Apply image filtering, edge detection, and feature extraction techniques
Implement object detection using YOLO, SSD, or Faster R-CNN
Perform image segmentation using U-Net and Mask R-CNN
Build face recognition and image classification systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Computer vision is used in autonomous vehicles, surveillance, medical imaging, augmented reality, and quality inspection.

subjects.name: Natural Language Processing
subjects.description: Study of computational techniques for processing and understanding human language. Covers text preprocessing, syntax parsing, sentiment analysis, machine translation, and transformer-based models.
learning_outcomes: Preprocess text data including tokenization, stemming, and lemmatization
Build sentiment analysis and text classification models
Implement sequence-to-sequence models for machine translation
Fine-tune large language models using Hugging Face transformers
subjects.industry_relevance: NLP powers chatbots, virtual assistants, search engines, and content analysis across media, healthcare, and customer service.

subjects.name: Neural Networks
subjects.description: Foundations of artificial neural networks including perceptrons, multi-layer networks, backpropagation, activation functions, regularization techniques, and training dynamics.
learning_outcomes: Implement perceptrons and multi-layer neural networks from scratch
Derive and apply backpropagation for training neural networks
Select appropriate activation functions and regularization techniques
Analyze gradient descent variants and optimization algorithms
subjects.industry_relevance: Neural networks form the backbone of modern AI systems used in image recognition, speech processing, and predictive analytics.

subjects.name: AI Ethics
subjects.description: Study of ethical considerations in AI development and deployment including bias, fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and societal impact of autonomous systems.
learning_outcomes: Identify sources of bias in ML models and training data
Apply fairness metrics to evaluate algorithmic decisions
Design AI systems with transparency and explainability
Evaluate privacy implications of AI systems and data collection
subjects.industry_relevance: AI ethics is increasingly critical as regulations like EU AI Act shape responsible AI deployment across industries.

subjects.name: Generative AI
subjects.description: Advanced study of generative models including GANs, VAEs, autoregressive models, diffusion models, and large language models. Covers text, image, and multimodal generation.
learning_outcomes: Train and evaluate GANs for image generation
Implement diffusion models for high-quality image synthesis
Fine-tune large language models for domain-specific tasks
Build multimodal AI systems combining text and image generation
subjects.industry_relevance: Generative AI is revolutionizing content creation, drug discovery, design, and software development with tools like GPT, DALL-E, and Stable Diffusion.

subjects.name: Robotics
subjects.description: Introduction to robotics covering kinematics, dynamics, control systems, sensor integration, path planning, and robot programming with AI-based perception and decision making.
learning_outcomes: Solve forward and inverse kinematics for robotic manipulators
Implement path planning algorithms including A* and RRT
Integrate sensors for perception and localization
Program robots using ROS for autonomous navigation
subjects.industry_relevance: Robotics is transforming manufacturing, logistics, healthcare, and service industries with intelligent automation.

subjects.name: Pattern Recognition
subjects.description: Study of statistical and machine learning approaches for pattern classification. Covers Bayesian decision theory, maximum likelihood estimation, feature selection, and ensemble methods.
learning_outcomes: Apply Bayesian decision theory for optimal classification
Implement feature extraction and dimensionality reduction techniques
Design classifier ensembles using boosting and bagging
Evaluate classifier performance using ROC curves and confusion matrices
subjects.industry_relevance: Pattern recognition is essential for biometrics, speech recognition, medical diagnosis, and quality control in manufacturing.

subjects.name: Python for AI
subjects.description: Python programming focused on AI/ML applications. Covers NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Scikit-learn, and TensorFlow fundamentals. Emphasizes data manipulation, analysis, and model building.
learning_outcomes: Manipulate data using NumPy arrays and Pandas DataFrames
Create visualizations using Matplotlib and Seaborn
Implement ML pipelines using Scikit-learn
Build and train simple neural networks with TensorFlow
subjects.industry_relevance: Python is the primary language for AI/ML development used by data scientists and ML engineers globally.

subjects.name: Data Structures for AI
subjects.description: Data structures with emphasis on AI applications including trees, graphs, heaps, hash tables, and specialized structures for knowledge representation and search algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Implement tree and graph structures for AI problem representation
Use hash tables and heaps for efficient data access
Design data structures for knowledge representation systems
Analyze space-time tradeoffs for AI data structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Efficient data structures are critical for AI systems processing large datasets and complex knowledge representations.

subjects.name: Databases for AI
subjects.description: Database systems tailored for AI workflows covering SQL, NoSQL, vector databases, data warehouses, and data lakes with focus on storing and querying AI training data.
learning_outcomes: Design relational schemas for ML training datasets
Query vector databases for similarity search in AI applications
Implement ETL pipelines for AI data preparation
Manage large-scale datasets using distributed storage systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Database expertise is essential for ML pipeline engineering and managing training/inference data at scale.
